# Lx8 Labs

> A small research-and-engineering studio in the Bell Labs lineage. Built around the intersections of DevOps, AI, and theoretical physics. Founded 2013, based in Berlin.

The site is the studio's public surface. Four products (Tupã IDE, aimem, Algorithm Lab, Light/Gravity/Time), open research, hands-on engineering courses, and fixed-scope consulting for teams that take operability seriously.

Stack is intentionally simple: vanilla HTML + CSS + JavaScript, Three.js r128 from CDN, no build step. Deployed via GitHub Pages from the public `LX8/lx8-website` repository. GDPR-compliant fonts via Bunny.

## Pages

- [Home](https://lx8.dev/): Studio hero, stats strip, partners, pillars, about, courses preview, Algorithm Lab feature, projects, consulting, theory, insights, footer
- [Products](https://lx8.dev/products.html): IA hub with orbital diagram — four artifacts of one small studio (Tupã, aimem, Algorithm Lab, Light/Gravity/Time)
- [Team](https://lx8.dev/team.html): "Small team. Long horizons." How Lx8 Labs is run — studio philosophy, founder card, "specialists per engagement" working model
- [Research](https://lx8.dev/research.html): Open research surface — bipartite Einstein–Maxwell (under peer review at Foundations of Physics), Algorithm Lab as history-of-CS pedagogy, aimem as memory-architecture implementation
- [Tupã IDE](https://lx8.dev/tupa.html): A native macOS development environment (Swift + Rust + Metal). Multi-language, AI-native, wearables-aware. In active private development.
- [aimem](https://lx8.dev/aimem.html): Personal AI memory system. One canonical markdown source, per-tool adapters built from it. CoALA taxonomy, MCP-native, 42% token reduction. Open source at github.com/LeKCei/ai-memory.
- [Algorithm Lab](https://lx8.dev/algorithms.html): Three-column 3D visualizer — sidebar (chronological or by-category view, search filter), Three.js scene, permanent code panel with synchronized line highlighting + live STATE inspector. 39 algorithms, 7 categories, keyboard shortcuts (Space/R/→/↑↓/C/⌘K/+−/?)
- [Light, Gravity & Time](https://lx8.dev/theory.html): 5-scene Three.js exploration of the bipartite reading of Einstein–Maxwell. Companion to the paper submitted to Foundations of Physics (Springer Nature) in May 2026.
- [Courses](https://lx8.dev/courses.html): Six engineering courses from the studio — Kubernetes for Production, AI in Production, Cloud Architecture (AWS+GCP), DevSecOps Practitioner, DevOps Foundations, Theoretical Physics for Engineers
- [About (founder)](https://lx8.dev/about.html): Long-form bio for Alexei Ferreira — career timeline 2013→2026, stack, what I write about, the bipartite-theory physics work. The studio overview lives on /team.
- [Now](https://lx8.dev/now.html): What the studio is currently working on, reading, watching. Following the nownownow.com convention. Updated monthly.
- [Changelog](https://lx8.dev/changelog.html): Every shipped update on lx8.dev in reverse chronological order. Subscribe via the RSS feed for new entries.
- [Insights: Building a 3D algorithm visualizer in pure HTML](https://lx8.dev/insights/3d-algorithm-visualizer.html): Engineering article — single-file HTML, no framework, Three.js from CDN.
- [Insights: 13 years of DevOps consulting · eight lessons](https://lx8.dev/insights/13-years-consulting-lessons.html): Consulting article — sell outcomes, charge for thinking, hire your replacement.
- [Unity 6 placeholder](https://lx8.dev/unity.html): WebGL placeholder that auto-detects and loads a build dropped at `unity-build/`

## What the studio does

- **Consulting:** DevOps audits, Kubernetes operability, AI feature productionization, platform reviews. Fixed-scope projects, not retainers.
- **Courses:** Six self-paced + live cohorts across DevOps, Cloud, AI, Security, and Physics for engineers.
- **Products:** Tupã IDE (native macOS, in development). Tooling for engineers who care about latency, privacy, and not being interrupted by their editor.
- **Research:** Open papers on a bipartite reading of Einstein–Maxwell. Currently under peer review at Foundations of Physics. AlexeiFerreira corpus on GitHub.

## Optional

- [GitHub organization (LX8)](https://github.com/LX8): Source of the website + product code
- [LinkedIn (Alexei Ferreira)](https://de.linkedin.com/in/lx8-alexei): Founder profile
- [Insights RSS feed](https://lx8.dev/feed.xml): Subscribe to new articles
- [Sitemap](https://lx8.dev/sitemap.xml)
- [Manifest](https://lx8.dev/manifest.json): PWA installable
- [Impressum](https://lx8.dev/impressum.html): Legal notice (TMG § 5, German law)
- [Privacy Policy / Datenschutzerklärung](https://lx8.dev/privacy.html): GDPR-compliant data policy
